souvenirs.title: A small specimen jar
souvenirs.detail: It sits in the meadow grass now, a keepsake from your first real survey. Three living things went into the record, and the Sanctuary has felt different since.

souvenirs.title: A worn field notebook
souvenirs.detail: It rests against a woodland log, its pages soft from use. The park turned out to be a whole ecosystem, and you walked it the way a researcher would.

souvenirs.title: A chalk mark from the curb
souvenirs.detail: A small chalk mark from the day the sidewalk became a study site. The city was alive the whole time, and now the urban zone remembers you noticed.

souvenirs.title: A schoolyard survey card
souvenirs.detail: A little card from the spot you pass every day. You documented something new there, and quiet corners look different once you have really seen one.

souvenirs.title: A pebble from no place special
souvenirs.detail: A smooth pebble from wherever you happened to be standing. There is no boring place, and this small stone came back as proof.

souvenirs.title: A folding hand lens
souvenirs.detail: It waits near the soil, folded shut for now. You surveyed the ground at bug scale, and smaller worlds kept opening the closer you looked.

souvenirs.title: A pressed wildflower
souvenirs.detail: A bloom pressed flat to remember your pollinator patrol by. You traced the flowers to the workers, and the meadow hums a little more now.

souvenirs.title: A seed from a sidewalk crack
souvenirs.detail: A tough little seed from the toughest neighbors on the block. The urban zone keeps it as a reminder that life holds on wherever it lands.

souvenirs.title: A corner-to-corner map
souvenirs.detail: A small hand-drawn map of the whole grounds. Ecologists call that kind of survey a census, and this one is finished, folded, and kept.

souvenirs.title: A kite tail of field tags
souvenirs.detail: Four paper tags ride the tail of a small kite, one for each brand-new species from a single outing. The sky keeps it drifting overhead.
